body: Hey Sasha - looking at the NRS scale labels in the current pacing ring. 0-10 with only 'no pain' and 'worst pain' anchors isn't accessible for participants with low health literacy. Can we add mid-range descriptors like 'mild,' 'moderate,' 'severe'?

Frank - excellent catch. I've logged this as cohort action item in Linear. Willa will pick it up in the next sprint. Agree that WCAG-level accessibility language is a priority, especially for our participant-facing app.

Perfect. Also worth referencing the NPRS validation literature - Farrar et al. 2001 shows that a 2-point change on a 0-10 scale is professionally significant. Could be useful for the threshold algorithm.

Feature request: the pacing ring should allow variable daily baselines, not just a fixed threshold. My clients with fibromyalgia have genuinely different capacity on different days - a static threshold punishes them on bad days.

This is fantastic professional context, Frank. This is now cohort action item in our roadmap. We're targeting the adaptive baseline for Q3. Your framing around 'capacity variability' versus 'pain variability' is exactly right.

Frank - putting together the Q1 Chapter Advisory agenda for the July 14 call. Want your professional input on three items: (1) participant cohort intake steps, (2) partner dashboard v2, (3) Occupational Therapy integration API spec. Can you come prepared with 2-3 notes per item?

Absolutely. I'll have notes ready by June 30. For the cohort intake steps - I'd strongly recommend a pain history intake that's occupation-based, not symptom-based. What activities matter to the participant is the right entry point.
We're rolling out the goal measure module to our outpatient team at St. Patrick. Is there a recommended order for onboarding clinicians versus enabling it participant-facing?

Hi Hannah - train clinicians on scoring first, then enable participant-facing intake once they've each done two practice administrations. We have a 20-minute walkthrough I can send. Closing this out, reopen anytime.
The bulk import keeps failing with a column mismatch. We're using your template but it flags a 7th column we don't have. Can you take a look?
Owen - there's a trailing comma on your header row creating a phantom column. Remove it and re-upload. We're also hardening the parser to ignore trailing delimiters in the next release.
Our pain clinic would like the professional references behind the pacing thresholds published in the help center. Readers and surveyors both ask.
Great request, Sofia. We've added the NPRS validation references - including Farrar et al. 2001 on the 2-point professionally significant change - to the pacing module docs. Live now.
Goniometry values look fine on screen but the exported PDF leaves the range-of-motion table blank. Happens on every coach account here.
Reproduced internally - the PDF renderer isn't pulling the hand-module ROM fields. Logged as cohort action item, engineering investigating. Keeping conversation open until the patch ships.
We offboarded two clinicians last month but billing still shows the old seat count. Can you reconcile this before our next invoice?
Done, Lisa - seat count corrected and your next invoice will prorate the difference. Apologies for the lag in the sync. Marking this resolved.
We're evaluating outcome-measurement platforms for our therapy group. Could we set up a walkthrough focused on goal measure and home-program tracking?

Hi Diane - we found the CEU certificate 404 was a broken template path after last week's deploy. Fixed and backfilled certificates for everyone who completed the goal measure course. They can re-download now.
Recorded remote coaching sessions aren't showing up in the documentation tab after the visit ends. We need them saved for our charting compliance. Can you check the recording pipeline?
